name: Laredo-Webb Neighborhood Housing Services
description_short: NeighborWorks Laredo is a HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and financial counseling since 1991.
description_long:
NeighborWorks Laredo has operated as a community development organization in Laredo, Texas since 1991, focused on expanding homeownership and financial stability for low-to-moderate-income families. The organization is part of the NeighborWorks network and employs HUD-certified instructors to deliver counseling and education services. Their approach combines classroom instruction, online courses, and personalized one-on-one guidance to prepare consumers for homeownership.

The organization offers free homebuyer education courses (both in-person and online), foreclosure prevention counseling, credit improvement guidance, and rental property education. They provide personalized guidance sessions to help consumers navigate the homebuying process and connect with lending opportunities. Their online courses allow self-paced learning for those unable to attend Saturday in-person sessions or Zoom classes. All homebuyer courses are offered at no cost ($0.00).

NeighborWorks Laredo is distinguished by its impressive track record: over 3,625 families have become homeowners through their program, with $401.3 million invested in the community and 561 foreclosures prevented. They maintain partnerships with community organizations and actively support new affordable housing development, including the Three Points Village project offering 24 attainable homes. Their HUD certification ensures compliance with fair housing standards and federal lending guidelines.

The main limitation is geographic specificity—services are primarily designed for Laredo area residents. The organization relies on donor support and partnerships rather than generating revenue, which may affect scalability. While they provide comprehensive counseling, they do not directly issue loans or repair credit reports; instead, they prepare consumers to work with lenders and manage credit independently.
pros: HUD-certified instructors deliver homebuyer education meeting federal standards
Completely free homebuyer courses (in-person, Zoom, and online options)
Documented impact: 3,625+ homeowners created and $401.3M invested in community
Foreclosure prevention counseling available to help at-risk homeowners
Self-paced online courses accommodate busy schedules without Saturday availability
Personalized one-on-one guidance sessions beyond classroom instruction
Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (FHEO) compliance explicitly referenced
cons: Limited to Laredo, Texas geographic area; not accessible to consumers in other regions
In-person courses frequently at full capacity (April 2026 class listed as full) with limited enrollment
Does not directly provide loans, credit repair, or credit monitoring services
No information provided about response times, counselor availability, or appointment scheduling
Reliance on donor funding may limit expansion of services or program continuity
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Laredo preparing for mortgage applications
Homeowners facing foreclosure seeking prevention counseling and options
Low-to-moderate-income families seeking free financial education before purchasing
Consumers wanting to understand fair housing rights and lending practices
diagnosis: NeighborWorks Laredo is best for Laredo-area first-time homebuyers and at-risk homeowners seeking free, HUD-certified education and counseling to prepare for homeownership. The main caveat is geographic limitation—this organization serves only the Laredo region and does not provide direct lending, credit repair, or credit monitoring services; it functions as an educational and advisory resource within a broader homebuying ecosystem.
services: Free homebuyer education courses (in-person Saturday classes)
Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity (FHEO) information and resources
Lead-based paint hazard disclosure education (EPA & HUD compliance)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: NeighborWorks Laredo is a HUD-certified non-profit providing free homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and financial counseling.
